properties.tree_description:
Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Confirmed large basal cavity present. Large scaffold branch has failed and ripped back to the main stem with visible decay.
		
		
			Tree number:3Species:Black Cherry Tree Size (DBH):27Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Heavily covered in English ivy. Competing leader failure with tear-out extending back to the main trunk. Ground inspection indicates decay extending into the trunk. Sounding at the rear of the basal area indicates hidden internal decay. Cavity present on a buttress root and sloughing bark observed on the rear of the trunk.
		
		
			Tree number:4Species:Pecan Tree Size (DBH):9Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Heavily covered in English ivy. Codominant V-shaped leader present, possibly developed to avoid a previous dead or failed branch site from Tree 3. Structural root plate appears shared/deformed in association with Tree 3.
		
		
			Tree number:5Species:Pecan Tree Size (DBH):20Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Growing within five feet of an occupied structure.
		
		
			Tree number:6Species:Water Oak Tree Size (DBH):17Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Stem exhibits a pronounced dog-leg with woundwood at the union associated with a historic canopy failure. Remote imagery review indicates that a nearby tree failure around 2012 likely removed approximately 50% of this tree’s canopy. Current structure reflects compensatory growth following that loss, with a decayed limb remnant and altered stem architecture. Unusual callus tissue present on the rear of the trunk. Audiovisual mounting hardware embedded in the lower trunk.
